author: Artie Tsoy
description: Link Link! finds semantically similar notes and shows them in a side panel. Click to open, drag into the editor to paste a [[link]], or batch-interconnect your entire vault in one command! - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Artie Tsoy
manifest.description: `Link Link!` finds semantically similar notes and shows them in a live side panel, ranked by relevance. Click to open, drag into the editor to paste a [[link]], or batch-connect your entire vault in one command.
manifest.name: Link Link

name: Link Link

releases.author: Artieficr
releases.description:
# Added features
## Live Mode
Now you can see semantically related notes to text you're typing in real time. It might get handy when you need to see related notes not to the whole active note, but to a specific segment. `Selection Mode` was doing almost the same job, but now you don't need to select text at all. It tracks either your current paragraph or the last N words before the cursor, and re-embeds it automatically a short pause after you stop typing.

When enabled (Settings → Additional Modes → Live Mode), a corresponding button appears in the side panel header to turn the mode on and off.
# QOL changes
- `Clear related field` command now informs, that exceptions are not touched.
- Side panel now shows backlinks and outgoing links even if an active note is not indexed. You can still index note by either clicking the `index note` button in the list view or the central node in the graph view (it now has warning triangle if note is not indexed).
- `Selection Mode` tab is renamed to `Additional Modes` and now shows both *Selection* and *Live* modes.
- Switch from display() to getSettingDefinitions() so you can search for settings globally.
# Bug fixes
- Side panel index button was indexing the whole vault instead of only indexing active note.
- In `Selection mode` Graph nodes did not display outgoing/backlinks indicators.
releases.name: Release v1.5.0

releases.author: Artieficr
releases.description:
Obsidian review did not like a few code formatting from 1.4.0. Now they fixed.
Doubling description of 1.4.0 below.

# Added features
## Selection Mode
Run a semantic search on any selected text passage — not just the active note.

When enabled (Settings → Selection Mode), a highlighter button appears in the panel header. Select 5 or more words in any note, click the button, and the panel switches to showing notes semantically related to that passage. Click the button again to return to the normal view.
### How it works
_Only works in Editor mode._

- The selected text is embedded on the fly using your configured model
- Results use the same threshold and Top N settings by default, but can be configured to use different ones.

**Selection entry (list view)**: A collapsible card at the top shows the source note and a preview of the selected text. Click it to open the Selection Text Popup.

**Selection node (graph view)**: Click the center node it to open the Selection Text Popup.

**Selection Text Popup**: Shows the full selected text (selectable, with a Copy button) and a Find button that opens the original passage.
# QOL changes
- Center node color is now configurable
releases.name: Release v1.4.1

releases.name: Release v1.4.0

releases.author: Artieficr
releases.description:
- **Security**: updated protobufjs to 7.x (resolves several upstream advisories in the ML model loading pipeline)
- **Build**: the bundled ONNX runtime no longer has access to the filesystem — model files are fetched from CDN only
- **Code**: resolved TypeScript warnings flagged during Obsidian plugin review
releases.name: Release v1.3.1

releases.author: Artieficr
releases.description:
# Migration to CDN workflow
To comply with Obsidian Plugin reviewer requirements the embedding model runtime (WASM files) is no longer bundled with the plugin: it only allows to use 3 standard files: `Main.js`, `manifest.json` and `styles.css`. Instead, embedding model downloads automatically from a CDN the first time you use semantic search. After that first load, everything is cached locally: subsequent Obsidian restarts are unaffected and require no additional downloads, plugin runs offline.

This reduces the plugin's install size by ~19 MB.

## What to expect on first use after updating
On the first time Link Link! loads the embedding model (when you open the side panel or run "Index vault"), it will fetch the runtime from the internet. This is a one-time download. After that, nothing changes.

# Bug fixes & code quality
- Fixed several TypeScript warnings flagged by Obsidian's plugin reviewer
- Improved CSS specificity so styles don't rely on !important.
- Releases are now built reproducibly from source via CI/CD and include artifact attestations so you can verify the download came from this repo.
releases.name: Release v1.3.0

releases.author: Artieficr
releases.description:
# Code quality & compatibility

- Minimum Obsidian version bumped to 1.7.2. This is required to support the panel-reveal behavior. 
- Removed all direct DOM innerHTML writes in favor of Obsidian's safe DOM API — a requirement for plugin store compliance.
- Eliminated unnecessary any type casts throughout the codebase — all internal types are now explicit.
- Fixed several unhandled promise warnings.
- Removed leftover @ts-ignore directives that suppressed TypeScript checks on public Obsidian APIs.

No user-facing behavior changes.
releases.name: Release v1.2.3

releases.author: Artieficr
releases.description: Claude automated code clean up based on feedback from Obsidian plugin publishing process.
releases.name: Release v1.2.2

releases.author: Artieficr
releases.description:
# QOL changes
- Improved interconnect notification text.
- Optimised `Clear related field` command based on Obsidian cache file.

# Bug fixes
- Returned indexation progress bar to the right place in the settings.
- Actualised tooltip text for `B` badge in the list view.
releases.name: Release v1.2.1

releases.author: Artieficr
releases.description:
# Added features
## Setup Wizard
First-time setup now walks you through everything in one place and helps to understand how plugin works.
### What the wizard covers:
- **Model** — pick what model you want to use: `built-in` (ready to use out of box), `Ollama provided` or use an `existing index` file from another plugin if you have one (e.g. [Copilot](https://github.com/logancyang/obsidian-copilot)).
- **Indexing scope** — choose which folders and files to include or exclude before the first index run, so you're not embedding templates, attachments, or any other noise.
- **Index your vault** — runs indexing right inside the wizard with a live progress bar. You can skip and complete index later. But better run it early.
- **Interlink** — Obsidian was made for meaningful note connections. Make one right during setup with guided instructions. Check updated Graph view right afterwards.

The wizard opens automatically on first install and can be re-run any time from Settings or via the command palette (Run setup wizard).

# QOL changes
- Added visual badges: `O` for Outgoing links and `B` for Backlinks.
- Added manual side panel refresh button for edits that may not be picked up automatically.
- Removed Interlink Vault button from the side panel — it remains available in plugin settings and the command palette.

# Bug fixes
- Fixed bugs in handling of outgoing links and backlinks in both list and graph views.
- Fixed drag-and-drop from the side panel stopping after switching notes.
- Fixed bugs in the logic for finding and displaying semantically similar notes.
- fixed logic of ranking note by similarity score: backlinks and outgoing links were forced to the end of a list.
releases.name: Release v1.2.0

releases.author: Artieficr
releases.description:
- Fixed bugs in handling of outgoing links and backlinks in both list and graph views
- Added visual badges: `O` for Outgoing links and `B` for Backlinks
- Fixed drag-and-drop from the side panel stopping after switching notes
- Fixed drag-and-drop pasting to the wrong position — now inserts at the current cursor position, instead of last remembered
- Added manual side panel refresh button for edits that may not be picked up automatically
- Fixed bugs in the logic for finding and displaying semantically similar notes
- Removed Interlink Vault button from the side panel — it remains available in plugin settings and the command palette
releases.name: Release v1.1.0

releases.author: Artieficr
releases.description:
- Fix notification when running Interlink Vault from panel button
- Change default Top N from 30 to 15 to not overflood vault with low quality semantic connections
releases.name: Release v1.0.1

releases.author: Artieficr
releases.description:
**Link Link!** — first public release.

Semantic similarity panel powered by `bge-small-en-v1.5`
Copilot plugin index support — no double indexing if you already use it
List and graph views with color coded similarity markers
One-click connection icon to add or remove `related:` links
Interlink Vault command — batch-connect your entire vault in one run

**printf("hello world!\n")**
releases.name: Release v1.0.0
